Short CV

Born in Istanbul in 1985, Ali Cankut Tatlıparmak graduated from Istanbul Faculty of Medicine in 2012. He completed his specialty education at University of Health Sciences İstanbul Education and Research Hospital in 2018 with his thesis titled "The role of neutrophil lymphocyte ratio and blood viscosity in predicting rebleeding in patients with upper gastrointestinal bleeding admitted to the emergency department". He did his compulsory service at Arnavutköy Public Hospital and assumed the duty of Emergency Physician of the same hospital. After his public service, he worked at Şişli Kolan International Hospital and Memorial Şişli Hospital. He has articles on different fields of emergency medicine in many national and international indexed journals. He is married and father of one child and speaks English fluently.
